en: Therefore, while the promise of entering his rest still stands, let us fear lest any of you should seem to have failed to reach it.
zh: 所以，那進入安息的應許，既然還給我們留著，我們就應該戰戰兢兢，恐怕我們中間有人像是被淘汰了。

en: For good news came to us just as to them, but the message they heard did not benefit them, because they were not united by faith with those who listened.
zh: 因為有福音傳給我們，像傳給他們一樣，只是他們所聽見的道，對他們沒有益處，因為他們沒有用信心與所聽見的打成一片 。

en: For we who have believed enter that rest, as he has said, "As I swore in my wrath, 'They shall not enter my rest,'" although his works were finished from the foundation of the world.
zh: 然而我們信了的人，就可以進入那安息。正如 神所說： “我在烈怒中起誓說， 他們絕不可進入我的安息！” 其實 神的工作，從創立世界以來已經完成了。

en: For he has somewhere spoken of the seventh day in this way: "And God rested on the seventh day from all his works."
zh: 因為論到第七日，他在聖經某一處說：“在第七日 神歇了他的一切工作。”

en: And again in this passage he said, "They shall not enter my rest."
zh: 但在這裡又說：“他們絕不可進入我的安息。”

en: Since therefore it remains for some to enter it, and those who formerly received the good news failed to enter because of disobedience,
zh: 既然這安息還留著要讓一些人進去，但那些以前聽過福音的人，因為不順從不得進去；

en: again he appoints a certain day, "Today," saying through David so long afterward, in the words already quoted, "Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts."
zh: 所以 神就再定一個日子，就是過了很久以後，藉著大衛所說的“今天”，就像前面引用過的： “如果你們今天聽從他的聲音， 就不要硬著心。”

en: For if Joshua had given them rest, God would not have spoken of another day later on.
zh: 如果約書亞已經使他們享受了安息， 神後來就不會再提到別的日子了。

en: So then, there remains a Sabbath rest for the people of God,
zh: 這樣看來，為了 神的子民，必定另外有一個“安息日”的安息保留下來。

en: for whoever has entered God's rest has also rested from his works as God did from his.
zh: 因為那進入 神安息的人，就歇了自己的工作，好像 神歇了自己的工作一樣。

en: Let us therefore strive to enter that rest, so that no one may fall by the same sort of disobedience.
zh: 所以，我們要竭力進入那安息，免得有人隨著那不順從的樣子就跌倒了。

en: For the word of God is living and active, sharper than any two-edged sword, piercing to the division of soul and of spirit, of joints and of marrow, and discerning the thoughts and intentions of the heart.
zh: 因為 神的道是活的，是有效的，比一切兩刃的劍更鋒利，甚至可以刺入剖開魂與靈，關節與骨髓，並且能夠辨明心中的思想和意念。

en: And no creature is hidden from his sight, but all are naked and exposed to the eyes of him to whom we must give account.
zh: 被造的在 神面前沒有一樣不是顯明的，萬有在他的眼前都是赤露敞開的；我們必須向他交帳。

en: Since then we have a great high priest who has passed through the heavens, Jesus, the Son of God, let us hold fast our confession.
zh: 我們既然有一位偉大的、經過了眾天的大祭司，就是 神的兒子耶穌，就應該堅持所宣認的信仰。

en: For we do not have a high priest who is unable to sympathize with our weaknesses, but one who in every respect has been tempted as we are, yet without sin.
zh: 因為我們的大祭司並不是不能同情我們的軟弱，他像我們一樣，也曾在各方面受過試探，只是他沒有犯罪。

en: Let us then with confidence draw near to the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy and find grace to help in time of need.
zh: 所以，我們只管坦然無懼地來到施恩的寶座前，為的是要領受憐憫，得到恩惠，作為及時的幫助。
